findings.title: North Star Metric: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
findings.description: The North Star metric for CloudMetrics is Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR), which reflects the total revenue generated from all active subscriptions on a monthly basis. This metric will guide the overall health and growth of the business as it transitions to a consumption-based pricing model.
assumptions: Transitioning to consumption-based pricing will stabilize or increase MRR.
recommendations: Monitor MRR closely during the transition to ensure it meets targets.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Customer Churn Rate
findings.description: The customer churn rate measures the percentage of customers who cancel their subscriptions within a given period. This is critical to monitor during the pricing transition to understand customer retention.
assumptions: Churn rate will increase due to pricing changes.
recommendations: Implement proactive customer outreach for high-risk segments.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Net Promoter Score (NPS)
findings.description: NPS measures customer satisfaction and loyalty by asking customers how likely they are to recommend the service. Maintaining or improving NPS is essential during the pricing transition.
assumptions: NPS will remain stable or improve with better customer communication.
recommendations: Conduct regular NPS surveys to gauge customer sentiment.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: Customer Segmentation Data
findings.description: There is insufficient data on customer segments that will be most affected by the pricing changes. This gap could hinder targeted communication and risk management efforts.
assumptions: Customer segmentation data is critical for understanding churn risk.

evidence.excerpt: Finance and product need a model that identifies which customer segments will see cost increases vs. decreases.

recommendations: Develop a detailed customer segmentation analysis to inform pricing strategy.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Pricing Sensitivity Analysis
findings.description: Conduct an experiment to analyze customer sensitivity to pricing changes by offering different pricing tiers to select customer segments and measuring churn and engagement.
assumptions: Different pricing tiers will yield varying levels of customer retention.

evidence.excerpt: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to consumption-based pricing.

recommendations: Run A/B tests with different pricing models to gauge customer response.

summary: The analysis identifies the Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) as the North Star metric, with Customer Churn Rate as a primary KPI and Net Promoter Score (NPS) as a guardrail metric. A measurement gap in customer segmentation data was noted, alongside a recommendation for a pricing sensitivity analysis experiment to better understand customer reactions to the new pricing model.
